strengths: 6+ years requirement comfortably exceeded with 15+ years of full-stack engineering across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems, including senior and staff-level roles at Berlin tech companies

Direct hands-on experience with the exact primary stack — TypeScript, Node.js, React — at Optilyz, RIDE Capital and re:cinq, plus current AI-agent infrastructure work (MCP code generators, agentic workflow frameworks) matching the AI agent development requirement

Strong software craftsmanship background (TDD, pairing, CI/CD, trunk-based development at RIDE Capital), multi-language proficiency (TS, Java, Python, Kotlin-adjacent JVM, DLang, C/C++), MongoDB/SQL, Docker, Kubernetes, AWS CDK, plus a CS degree and a track record of teaching, talks and open-source leadership
concerns: Resume does not explicitly name Nest.js or Vitest, though the broader Node.js/TypeScript/Jest-style testing background strongly suggests rapid ramp-up
Kotlin is not explicitly listed in the skills section, though Java experience and polyglot history (DLang, C++, Swift) indicate easy transferability
Most recent enterprise-scale internal tooling experience is implied through platform and micro-services work rather than spelled out as 'internal automation tooling'

key_factors.factor: AI agent and MCP infrastructure experience
Seniority and craftsmanship discipline (TDD, CI/CD, architecture)

response.justification: The candidate decisively exceeds the 6+ year bar with a senior/staff trajectory in Berlin's engineering scene and direct, current production experience in the exact primary stack (TypeScript, Node.js, React) at Optilyz, RIDE Capital and re:cinq. Crucially, the rare AI agent development requirement is already being met today through MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks at re:cinq. Add a CS degree, polyglot fluency (TS, Java, Python, C/C++, DLang, Swift), MongoDB/SQL, Docker/Kubernetes, AWS CDK, plus a strong craftsmanship culture (TDD, pairing, trunk-based development, code katas, public talks), and this profile reads as an unusually well-aligned, low-risk fit for a senior IC building internal automation tooling.
